2604.04651.title: Search, Do not Guess: Teaching Small Language Models to Be Effective Search Agents
2604.04651.paper_summary: Agents equipped with search tools have emerged as effective solutions for knowledge-intensive tasks. While Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit strong reasoning capabilities, their high computational cost limits practical deployment for search agents. Consequently, recent work has focused on distilling agentic behaviors from LLMs into Small Language Models (SLMs). Through comprehensive evaluation on complex multi-hop reasoning tasks, we find that despite possessing less parametric knowledge, SLMs invoke search tools less frequently and are more prone to hallucinations. To address this issue, we propose \policy, a lightweight fine-tuning approach that explicitly trains SLMs to reliably retrieve and generate answers grounded in retrieved evidence. Compared to agent distillation from LLMs, our approach improves performance by 17.3 scores on Bamboogle and 15.3 scores on HotpotQA, achieving LLM-level results across benchmarks. Our further analysis reveals that adaptive search strategies in SLMs often degrade performance, highlighting the necessity of consistent search behavior for reliable reasoning.
2604.04651.paper_summary_zh: 配備搜尋工具的代理已成為知識密集型任務的有效解決方案。儘管大型語言模型（LLMs）展現出強大的推理能力，但其高計算成本限制了在搜尋代理中的實際部署。因此，最近的研究集中於將代理行為從LLMs提煉到小型語言模型（SLMs）中。通過對複雜的多跳推理任務進行全面評估，我們發現儘管SLMs擁有較少的參數知識，但它們較少調用搜尋工具，且更容易出現幻覺。為了解決這一問題，我們提出了\policy，一種輕量級的微調方法，明確訓練SLMs可靠地檢索和生成基於檢索證據的答案。與從LLMs進行的代理提煉相比，我們的方法在Bamboogle上提高了17.3分，在HotpotQA上提高了15.3分，實現了跨基準的LLM級別結果。我們的進一步分析顯示，SLMs中的自適應搜尋策略往往會降低性能，突顯出一致搜尋行為對於可靠推理的必要性。
2604.04651.author: Yizhou Liu et.al.
2604.04651.authors: Yizhou Liu, Qi Sun, Yulin Chen, Siyue Zhang, Chen Zhao
